Good, I can see the debug page. Let me analyze what I can see: 1. The page is showing "AI Aquarium — Debug Dashboard" 2. Objects section shows 10 objects: - Stove: idle, tile (3,3), 1/1 free - Fridge: idle, tile (2,3), 1/1 free (not visible, need to scroll) - Tea Set: idle, tile (5,5), **full** (1 pip filled) - Kotatsu: idle, tile (6,6), 2/2 free - TV: idle, tile (9,3), 2/2 free - Bookshelf: idle, tile (15,3), 1/1 free - Desk: idle, tile (14,5), 1/1 free - Bed: idle, tile (16,5), 1/1 free - Easel: idle, tile (2,7), 1/1 free - Bonsai: idle, tile (11,8), 1/1 free 3. For characters, I can see Maple is idle at position (6,7) facing down with full mood and energy at 100, and her AI will update in 26 ticks. Sol is walking toward the tea set at (6,4) facing left, while Nyx is idle at (10,6) facing down. Both have full stats and the same AI timing. Looking at the event log, Nyx just greeted Sol this morning and Sol responded by heading to make tea—the display names are showing correctly as Maple, Sol, and Nyx rather than their sprite identifiers. I notice the tea set is marked as "full" with a filled pip, yet Sol has it reserved and is walking toward it. The kotatsu and TV both show 2/2 capacity available. Let me speed up to 4x to watch how the characters make decisions over time and see if the tea set status changes as Sol approaches.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #11
Let me analyze what I can see so far: 1. **Objects**: - Stove: idle, 1/1 free ✓ - Fridge: idle, 1/1 free ✓ - Tea Set: ready, 1/1 free (44s remaining) - it's in "ready" state now - Kotatsu: idle, 2/2 free ✓ (multi-capacity) - TV: idle, 2/2 free ✓ (multi-capacity) - Bed: resting, **full** (56s remaining) - capacity-1 object being used - Easel: idle, 1/1 free ✓ - Bonsai: idle, 1/1 free ✓ 2. **Characters**: - Maple: interacting, position (16,4), facing right, target "bed (using)", mood 100, energy 100, hold 56s - Sol: idle, position (5,4), facing left, no target - Nyx: idle, position (11,7), facing down, no target 3. **Event Log**: - 8:21 AM: bonsai - tending → idle (auto) - 8: 19 AM: tea_set - brewing → ready (auto) - 8:16 AM: bed - idle → resting (auto) - 8:09 AM: bonsai - idle → tending (auto) - 8:04 AM: tea_set - idle → brewing (auto) - 8:04 AM: Maple - move → (8,7) "Time to start the day!" (AI) - 8:01 AM: Nyx - move → (10,6) "Good morning, Sol! Mind if I join you?" (AI) - 8:01 AM: Sol - interact → tea set "I could use a nice cup of tea to start the day." (AI) Looking at the event timeline, the characters are behaving naturally with their morning routines—Sol initiated tea brewing, Nyx greeted them, and Maple started moving around. The display names are working correctly, and the AI-generated dialogue feels contextual to their actions. I've confirmed the capacity system is functioning properly with the bed showing as full while Maple uses it, and the tea set tracking its free slots. Now I want to speed up the simulation to catch more complex interactions like characters actually talking to each other, how the system handles capacity conflicts, and whether fallback behaviors trigger when objects are unavailable.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #15
Good observations at 4x speed: 1. **Stove**: now "full" (someone is using it or it has a reserved state) 2. **Desk**: now "full" (Nyx is walking to desk with reserved target) 3. **Bed**: still "resting", full (31s remaining), Maple is interacting with bed 4. Both Sol and Nyx had "interact → tea set" decisions at 8:31 AM - interesting! Both wanted the same tea set (capacity 1) 5. Sol is now walking to stove (reserved), Nyx walking to desk (reserved) The event log shows: - 8:31 AM: Sol - interact → tea set "Time for my morning tea..." - 8:31 AM: Nyx - interact → tea set "I think I'll make myself a cup of tea..." - Then tea_set went ready → idle at 8:33 AM So both of them decided to use the tea set simultaneously, but since it only has capacity 1, one of them must have gotten priority or the system resolved the conflict somehow. Looking at where they are now—Sol heading to the stove and Nyx to the desk—it seems like they both abandoned the tea set interaction and moved on to different activities. I'm not seeing explicit REJECT or FALLBACK events in the log, so I need to scroll through the event history more carefully to understand how this conflict was actually resolved. Let me speed up the simulation and watch for more events to clarify what happened.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #21
Excellent! I can see a lot more now at 11:49 AM. Let me analyze: 1. **Kotatsu**: "cozy" state, 1/2 free (someone is using it!) - Sol is interacting with kotatsu (using). The kotatsu pip shows 1 filled, 1 empty = 1/2 free. This means 1 of 2 slots is occupied. This confirms multi-capacity (capacity 2) kotatsu is working. 2. **Sol**: interacting, position (6,5), facing left, target "kotatsu (using)", hold 43s - Sol is using the kotatsu! 3. **Nyx** is walking around at position (11,7) with no particular target in mind, while **Maple** is idle at (5,4) and will be doing something in about 8 seconds. Looking at the event log, I can see the timeline of recent activities—the bonsai finished tending earlier, the tea set became ready, and Maple had just decided to make some tea and relax. Going back further, I'm seeing more of the sequence: the bonsai started being tended, the kotatsu became cozy, Nyx stopped to admire the bonsai tree, Sol settled under the kotatsu for warmth, the desk work wrapped up, and the tea set went through its brewing cycle starting from this morning. I'm noticing the state transitions for objects like the tea set moving through brewing and ready states, and I can see the AI is making multiple interaction and movement decisions. I'm still waiting to observe talk handshaking with the TALKING state and purple badges, as well as fallback logic and scenarios where characters compete for or get blocked from limited-capacity objects—let me scroll through more of the event log to find these cases. The Kotatsu's capacity display showing 1/2 free with the filled indicator is solid confirmation that multi-capacity tracking is working correctly.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #24
I now have a comprehensive view of the event log. Let me analyze all the M1 criteria so far: 1. **Slot System (Smart Objects)**: - Objects show slot capacity: 1/1 free, 2/2 free ✓ - Kotatsu shows 1/2 free when one character is using it ✓ (multi-capacity=2) - TV shows 2/2 free ✓ (multi-capacity=2) - Stove shows "full" (capacity-1) ✓ - Bed showed "full" (capacity-1) ✓ - At 12:01 PM, Nyx joined Sol under the kotatsu, which is the multi-capacity test in action. But then at 12:02 PM, Maple also tried to interact with the kotatsu—I need to verify whether she was blocked since the capacity should be full at that point. Looking at the timeline more carefully: the kotatsu transitioned from cozy to idle at 12:03 PM, right after both Nyx and Maple's interaction attempts. This suggests the session ended, but I'm trying to figure out if both characters were actually using it simultaneously or if one got rejected. Earlier at 11:27 AM, Sol had initiated the kotatsu interaction, and it became cozy at 11:32 AM, so that session was already active when Nyx joined at 12:01 PM. a while." At 12:02 PM: Maple - interact → kotatsu At 12:03 PM: kotatsu cozy → idle So Sol was the first user (at 11:27 AM), Nyx joined (at 12:01 PM), Maple also tried to join (at 12:02 PM). Then at 12:03 PM the kotatsu ended (cozy → idle). But wait, the kotatsu has capacity 2, so it can hold Sol + Nyx = 2/2 (full). So Maple's interaction at 12:02 PM should have been blocked or queued. I don't see a FALLBACK event in the log for Maple being blocked from the kotatsu. Hmm, maybe the kotatsu session ends dynamically and Maple arrived when it was already idle. Looking at the talk handshaking, I don't see any explicit "talking" events in the log—just "interact" and "move" actions. And scanning through for fallback logic, I notice there are no FALLBACK events recorded at all, even at 8:31 AM when both Sol and Nyx decided to interact with the tea set but ended up at different locations instead. Looking at the display names, I see Maple, Sol, and Nyx consistently used throughout. The core engine is firing AI decisions and object state changes as expected. Now I'm examining the state machines for each object—stove, tea set, kotatsu, bed, bonsai, desk, bookshelf, and fridge all cycling through their expected states. Characters are holding their positions for the full interaction duration as intended. I should reset and observe more carefully for talk events and fallback behavior, since I haven't captured any talk handshaking yet and I want to see what happens when two characters compete for the same object capacity.

But then at 12:02 PM, Maple also tried to interact with the kotatsu\u2014I need to verify whether she was blocked since the capacity should be full at that point.\n\n   Looking at the timeline more carefully: the kotatsu transitioned from cozy to idle at 12:03 PM, right after both Nyx and Maple's interaction attempts. This suggests the session ended, but I'm trying to figure out if both characters were actually using it simultaneously or if one got rejected. Earlier at 11:27 AM, Sol had initiated the kotatsu interaction, and it became cozy at 11:32 AM, so that session was already active when Nyx joined at 12:01 PM. a while.\"\n   At 12:02 PM: Maple - interact \u2192 kotatsu\n   At 12:03 PM: kotatsu cozy \u2192 idle\n\n   So Sol was the first user (at 11:27 AM), Nyx joined (at 12:01 PM), Maple also tried to join (at 12:02 PM). Then at 12:03 PM the kotatsu ended (cozy \u2192 idle). \n\n   But wait, the kotatsu has capacity 2, so it can hold Sol + Nyx = 2/2 (full). So Maple's interaction at 12:02 PM should have been blocked or queued. I don't see a FALLBACK event in the log for Maple being blocked from the kotatsu. \n\n   Hmm, maybe the kotatsu session ends dynamically and Maple arrived when it was already idle. Looking at the talk handshaking, I don't see any explicit \"talking\" events in the log\u2014just \"interact\" and \"move\" actions. ASSISTANTMESSAGE #28
Excellent! I can see critical evidence right after the Reset: 1. **Talk Handshaking**: - 8:05 AM: Nyx - talk → Maple "Good morning, Maple! How are you today?" (AI) - 8:05 AM: Nyx - → Maple rejected (interacting) REJECT - Maple's Target shows "↔ Nyx" - suggesting they're in a talk handshake! Wait, the reject event says "→ Maple rejected (interacting)" - this might be Nyx trying to talk to Maple who was interacting. So Nyx made the talk decision and sent the message, but Maple rejected it because she was already interacting with someone else. Yet her target still shows the bidirectional connection with Nyx, which is puzzling—maybe the rejection happened but they're still somehow linked, or perhaps the events are being logged in an unexpected order. Looking at the event ordering, the REJECT event (uid=4_109) appears before the talk event (uid=4_113) in the log, which means the rejection actually happened after Nyx decided to talk to Maple—exactly matching the test scenario where someone tries to talk to a character who's already interacting. The fact that Maple's target still shows "↔ Nyx" suggests the interaction handshake was initiated but then blocked by the rejection. Now I'm checking the reset state to confirm all objects returned to idle with their free slots restored, and I want to take a closer look at Maple's visual state to understand what that bidirectional target indicator means.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #32
Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze: 1. **Current state at 9:18 AM**: - Maple: interacting, position (2,6), facing left, target "↔ Nyx", hold 25s - Sol: interacting, position (16,4), facing right, target "◆ bed (using)", hold 54s - Nyx: interacting, position (5,4), facing left, target "◆ tea set (using)", hold 7s Interesting! Maple's target is "↔ Nyx" - the ↔ symbol suggests a talk/exchange. So Maple and Nyx are... talking? But Nyx is showing "target ◆ tea set (using)". Wait, I'm seeing something odd here. Nyx is actively using the tea set while Maple's target points to Nyx for conversation—maybe Maple is queued up to talk to Nyx once the tea set interaction finishes, or perhaps the target notation means something different than I initially thought. Looking at the objects now: the tea set is brewing with 7 seconds left under Nyx, the bed is occupied by Sol for 54 more seconds, and the easel shows painting activity with 25 seconds remaining. But I'm confused about who's actually at the easel since Maple's target shows Nyx rather than the easel, even though Maple's position and the hold timer seem to suggest involvement with it. I think what's happening is Maple is currently using the easel (that's the 25-second hold), but has queued up a conversation with Nyx to happen afterward—the "↔" symbol indicates a scheduled talk rather than an active interaction. Looking at the event log, I can see the recent activity: the bed transitioned to resting mode automatically at 9:12, the tea set started brewing at 9:10, and Nyx initiated the tea set interaction at 9:06 with a comment about having tea. Going further back, the bonsai was being tended to—Maple interacted with it at 8:58, and before that Sol had examined it at 8:48. The easel started painting automatically at 8:57, and there's a pattern of the bonsai cycling between tending and idle states. The tea set was ready at 8:38 and then went idle, and Nyx had started another tea set interaction at 8:36. The event log seems to cut off around 8:20, so I need to look at the earlier snapshot to see what happened around 8:05 when the REJECT and talk events occurred. This appears to be a fresh reset at 8:00 AM, so I should check the previous snapshot to understand the full sequence of events. Looking more closely at the current state, Maple is at the easel with 25 seconds remaining on a painting task, but her target shows "↔ Nyx" with a matching 25-second hold timer—suggesting she might have queued Nyx as a talk target while still painting, or the talk interaction is somehow overlapping with her easel use. Meanwhile, Nyx is occupied with the tea set at a different location, so either the talk attempt failed or there's a queued interaction I'm not fully seeing yet. Let me wait for Sol's AI decision to trigger (it's at 0 seconds) and check the JavaScript console to get more detailed event logs about what's actually happening with these interactions.

(1 args)\nmsgid=28 [log] [Maple] Maple walks over to the tea set and prepares a hot cup of tea, enjoying the comforting warmth and aroma. (1 args)\nmsgid=29 [log] [Nyx] Nyx greets Maple and starts a conversation. (1 args)\nmsgid=30 [log] [Maple] Maple walks over to the kotatsu and sits down, enjoying the warmth and company. (1 args)\nmsgid=31 [log] [Sol] You move over to the kotatsu and sit down, pulling the warm blanket over your legs. The soft, radiant heat from the table is soothing and relaxing. (1 args)\nmsgid=32 [log] [Nyx] Nyx walks over to the bookshelf and selects a book to read. (1 args)\nmsgid=33 [log] [Sol] You decide to make yourself a warm cup of tea at the tea set. (1 args)\nmsgid=34 [log] [Maple] Maple opens the fridge and peers inside, looking for a snack. (1 args)\nmsgid=35 [log] [Nyx] Greet Maple and invite her to have tea together. (1 args)\nmsgid=36 [log] [Sol] I walk over to the tea set and prepare a steaming hot cup of green tea. The fragrant aroma fills the air as I take a sip, feeling refreshed and ready to take on the day. (1 args)\nmsgid=37 [log] [Maple] I approach the bonsai tree and carefully examine its intricate branches and lush green foliage. The delicate nature of the plant captivates me, and I'm drawn to the sense of tranquility it exudes. (1 args)\nmsgid=38 [log] [Nyx] Nyx walks over to the tea set and interacts with it to brew a fresh cup of tea. (1 args)\nmsgid=39 [log] [Sol] I'll tend to the bonsai tree to make sure it's healthy and thriving. (1 args)\nmsgid=40 [log] [Maple] Maple approaches the bonsai and gently prunes and waters it, tending to its needs. (1 args)\nmsgid=41 [log] [Nyx] Nyx walks over to the tea set and prepares a warm cup of tea. (1 args)\nmsgid=42 [log] [Nyx] I will interact with the tea set to prepare some tea. (1 args)\nmsgid=43 [log] [Maple] Maple approaches the tea set and prepares a cup of tea. (1 args)\nmsgid=44 [log] [Sol] You walk over to the TV and turn it on, settling in to enjoy some programming. (1 args)\nmsgid=45 [log] [Maple] You decide to make yourself a nice cup of tea using the tea set. (1 args)\nmsgid=48 [log] [Nyx] Nyx stands still, taking a deep breath and appreciating the calm atmosphere of the cozy Japanese home. (1 args)"
